sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Income Tax Reference Application Nos. 386 & 387 of 2024 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For order on office objection Nos. 04, 17 & 27. 2. For order on CMA No. 2877/24. (Exemption) 3. For hearing of main case. 24.11.2025 Mr. S. Ahsan Ali Shah, advocate for applicant. On the last date, following order was passed:- “17.11.2025 Syed Ahsan Ali Shah, advocate for applicant. Learned counsel is not prepared and seeks time. At his request, to come up on 24.11.2025. Office is instructed to place copy of this order in the connected files.” Today, again time is being sought for no cogent reason whatsoever. It appears that the applicant is not interested in proceeding with this matter, therefore, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, these references are dismissed for non- prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Office is instructed to place copy of this order in the connected file. Judge Judge Ayaz p.s.
